title: अच्युताष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अच्युताष्टकम् ॥

अच्युतं केशवं रामनारायणं
कृष्णदामोदरं वासुदेवं हरिम्।
श्रीधरं माधवं गोपिकावल्लभं
जानकीनायकं रामचन्द्रं भजे॥ १॥

अच्युतं केशवं सत्यभामाधवं
माधवं श्रीधरं राधिकाराधितम्।
इन्दिरामन्दिरं चेतसा सुन्दरं
देवकीनन्दनं नन्दजं सन्दधे॥ २॥

विष्णवे जिष्णवे शंखिने चक्रिणे
रुक्मिणीराग्ने जानकीजानये।
वल्लवीवल्लभायाऽर्चिताया-ऽतुलं
धामने कामने योगिने ते नमः॥ ३॥

कृष्ण गोविन्द हे राम नारायण
श्रीपते वासुदेवाजित श्रीनिधे।
अच्युतानन्त हे माधवाधोक्षज
द्वारकानायक द्रौपदीरक्षक॥ ४॥

राक्षसक्षोभितः सीतया शोभितो
दण्डकारण्यभूपुण्यतां राघवः।
लक्ष्मणेनान्वितो वानरैः सेवितो-
गस्त्यसम्पूजितो राघवः पातु माम्॥ ५॥

धेनुकारिष्टहा निष्कृतो दावतो
गोपिकास्तोमपः कंसहा ते नमः।
कुञ्जवासी कुरुक्षेत्रवासी हते-
हन्त दुर्योधनं ते नमो वासव॥ ६॥

विद्युदुद्योतवत् प्रस्फुरद्वाससं
प्रावृडम्भोदवत् प्रोल्लसद्विग्रहम्।
वन्यया मालया शोभितोरःस्थलं
लोहिताङ्घ्रिद्वयं वारिजाक्षं भजे॥ ७॥

कुञ्चितैः कुन्तलैर्भ्राजमानाननं
रत्नमौलिं लसत्कुण्डलं गण्डयोः।
हारकेयूरकं कङ्कणप्रोज्ज्वलं
किङ्किणीमञ्जुलं श्यामलं नौमि हम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री अच्युताष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Achyutashtakam ||

Achyutam Keshavam Rama-Narayanam
Krishna-Damodaram Vasudevam Harim |
Shridharam Madhavam Gopika-vallabham
Janaki-nayakam Ramachandram bhaje || 1 ||

Achyutam Keshavam Satyabhama-dhavam
Madhavam Shridharam Radhika-radhitam |
Indira-mandiram chetasa sundaram
Devaki-nandanam Nandajam sandadhe || 2 ||

Vishnave Jishnave shankhine chakrine
Rukmini-ragne Janaki-janaye |
Vallavi-vallabhaya-architaya-atulam
Dhamane kamane yogine te namah || 3 ||

Krishna Govinda he Rama Narayana
Shripate Vasudeva-ajita Shrinidhe |
Achyutananta he Madhava-adhokshaja
Dvaraka-nayaka Draupadi-rakshaka || 4 ||

Rakshasa-kshobhitah Sitaya shobhito
Dandakaranya-bhu-punyatam Raghavah |
Lakshmanena-anvito vanaraiah sevito-
Agastya-sampujito Raghavah patu mam || 5 ||

Dhenukarishtaha nishkrito davato
Gopika-stomapah Kamsaha te namah |
Kunja-vasi Kurukshetra-vasi hate-
Hanta Duryodhanam te namo Vasava || 6 ||

Vidyud-udyotavat prasphurad-vasasam
Pravrid-ambhodavat prollasad-vigraham |
Vanyaya malaya shobhitor-ahsthalam
Lohitanghri-dvayam varijaksham bhaje || 7 ||

Kunchitaih kuntalair-bhrajamanananam
Ratna-maulim lasat-kundalam gandayoh |
Hara-keyurakam kankana-projjvalam
Kinkini-manjulam shyamalam naumi ham || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Achyutashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
अच्युत, केशव, राम, नारायण, कृष्ण, दामोदर, वासुदेव, हरि, श्रीधर, माधव, गोपिकाओं के वल्लभ, जानकी के नायक श्रीरामचन्द्र को मैं भजता हूँ॥ १॥

अच्युत, केशव, सत्यभामा के पति, माधव, श्रीधर, राधिका द्वारा आराधित, लक्ष्मी के मन्दिर, मन से सुन्दर, देवकी नन्दन, नन्द के पुत्र को मैं धारण करता हूँ॥ २॥

विष्णु, जिष्णु, शंख धारी, चक्र धारी, रुक्मिणी के अनुरागी, जानकी के जानने वाले, गोपियों के वल्लभ, अर्चित, अतुल, धामवान, कामपूर्ण, योगी — आपको नमस्कार॥ ३॥

हे कृष्ण, गोविन्द, हे राम, नारायण, श्रीपति, वासुदेव, अजित, श्रीनिधि, अच्युत, अनन्त, हे माधव, अधोक्षज, द्वारकानायक, द्रौपदी के रक्षक॥ ४॥

राक्षसों को क्षुब्ध करने वाले, सीता से शोभित, दण्डकारण्य को पुण्य करने वाले राघव, लक्ष्मण सहित, वानरों द्वारा सेवित, अगस्त्य द्वारा पूजित — वे राघव मेरी रक्षा करें॥ ५॥
benefits: Invokes the blessings of Lord Vishnu in all His forms
Traditionally associated with devotion and love towards the supreme lord
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and negative karma
Invokes peace of mind and spiritual joy
Traditionally invoked for protection from all dangers and evil influences
Invokes the blessing of liberation when recited with pure devotion
description: Achyutashtakam — अच्युताष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Traditional Vaishnavite Literature, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove all sins and negative karma as well as grant peace of mind and spiritual joy. Additionally, it is known to bestow liberation when recited with pure devotion. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi and Janmashtami for maximum spiritual benefit. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Achyutashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Achyutashtakam (Achyutam Keshavam) in Sanskrit with English transliteration and Hindi meaning. Popular 8-verse hymn to Lord Vishnu.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Achyutashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Achyutashtakam (अच्युताष्टकम्) — opening with the celebrated line Achyutam Keshavam Rama-Narayanam, Krishna-Damodaram Vasudevam Harim — is one of the most beloved eight-verse (ashtakam) Sanskrit hymns of the Vaishnava tradition, traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (c. 8th century CE). The hymn is a continuous garland of the supreme names of Lord Vishnu in his combined Krishna-Rama-Narayana form, celebrating the Lord as Achyuta (the never-falling, indestructible), Keshava (slayer of Keshi, the lord with beautiful hair), Madhava (lord of Lakshmi / consort of Maa Madhavi), Govinda (cowherd-protector), Damodara (whose belly was tied with a rope by Yashoda), Vasudeva (son of Vasudeva), Hari (the remover of sins), Shridhara (consort of Shri-Lakshmi), Janaki-nayaka (lord of Sita), Devaki-nandana (son of Devaki), Vishnu, Jishnu, Rakshasa-kshobhita Raghava, Dvaraka-nayaka, Draupadi-rakshaka, and many more. The hymn unifies all the Vishnu avataras — particularly Krishna and Rama — in a single continuous song. (Source: Adi Shankaracharya devotional corpus; classical Vaishnava tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Achyutam Keshavam Krishna Damodaram mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening line of the Achyutashtakam is a continuous garland of Vishnu's supreme names. Word-by-word: Achyutam — the never-falling, the indestructible, the one who never deviates from his devotees (a-chyuta = not fallen, not slipped); Keshavam — the lord with beautiful flowing hair (kesha = hair, va = possessor); also the slayer of the demon Keshi sent by Kamsa; also the trinity Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva (ka = Brahma, isha = Shiva, va = Vishnu — the one containing all three); Rama-Narayanam — Rama (the delightful one, lord of Sita) and Narayana (the resting-place of all beings, the cosmic ocean-floating supreme); Krishna — the dark-blue-complexioned attractor of all hearts (krish = to attract, na = bliss); Damodaram — whose belly (udara) was tied with a rope (dama) by Mata Yashoda when child Krishna broke the butter pot; Vasudevam — son of Vasudeva (Krishna's father), also the indweller of all (vasu) and divine (deva); Harim — the remover of sins, sufferings and ego (hri = to take away). The line is a complete Vishnu-name meditation. (Source: classical Vaishnava nama-japa tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Achyutashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Achyutashtakam is traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (c. 8th century CE) — the great Advaita acharya, founder of the four Amnaya Mathas (Sringeri, Dwarka, Puri, Jyotirmath), and author of innumerable prakarana granthas, bhashyas and bhakti stotras. Despite Shankara's primary identification with Advaita Vedanta, he composed numerous bhakti hymns to Vishnu, Krishna, Rama, Shiva, Devi, Ganesha and other deities, demonstrating his teaching that bhakti is the practical means by which the seeker prepares the mind for the supreme jnana of non-duality. His Vaishnava bhakti compositions include the Bhaja Govindam (Mohamudgara), Achyutashtakam, Krishnashtakam, Rama Bhujanga Prayata Stotra, Hari Stotra, Vishnu Bhujanga, Pandurangashtakam, Govindashtakam and many more. The Achyutashtakam is among his most frequently recited Krishna-Rama-Vishnu unified hymns, beloved across Smarta, Vaishnava and Bhakti traditions. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya bhakti corpus; Sringeri Sharada Peetham tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What names of Vishnu and Krishna appear in the Achyutashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Achyutashtakam is a remarkably dense compendium of Vishnu's supreme names. The eight verses include (verse 1) Achyuta, Keshava, Rama, Narayana, Krishna, Damodara, Vasudeva, Hari, Shridhara, Madhava, Gopika-vallabha, Janaki-nayaka, Ramachandra; (verse 2) Achyuta, Keshava, Satyabhama-dhava, Madhava, Shridhara, Radhika-aradhita, Indira-mandira, Devaki-nandana, Nanda-ja; (verse 3) Vishnu, Jishnu, Shankhin (conch-bearer), Chakrin (discus-bearer), Rukmini-ragna, Janaki-janaye, Vallavi-vallabha, Dhamana, Kamana, Yogin; (verse 4) Krishna, Govinda, Rama, Narayana, Shripate, Vasudeva, Ajita, Shri-nidhe, Achyuta-ananta, Madhava-adhokshaja, Dvaraka-nayaka, Draupadi-rakshaka; (verse 5) Raghava, Sita-shobhita, Lakshmana-anvita, Vanara-sevita, Agastya-sampujita; (verses 6-8) Dhenuka-arishta-ha, Gopika-stoma-pa, Kamsa-ha, Kunja-vasi, Kurukshetra-vasi, Duryodhana-han, Vasava, the lotus-eyed (varijaksha), Shyamala (the dark-blue lord), and many more. (Source: Achyutashtakam.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Achyutashtakam be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Achyutashtakam is most powerfully recited on Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — the supreme annual festival of Sri Krishna's birth), Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami — Sri Rama's birthday), all Ekadashi days (the supreme Vaishnava vrata day, twice every month), Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi — the gateway to Vaikuntha), Devasayani Ekadashi, Devotthani Ekadashi, the entire month of Kartik (Damodara Masa — the supreme Vaishnava month named after Damodara, one of the verse-names), Thursday (Guruvar — the weekly day of Vishnu) and Sunday (Ravivar — also auspicious for Vishnu). The hymn is recited daily by Vaishnavas of all sampradayas (Sri, Brahma, Rudra, Sanaka), Smartas, Gaudiya Vaishnavas, ISKCON devotees and any Hindu seeking blessings of Krishna-Rama-Vishnu. It is especially popular as a morning or evening sandhya recitation, as part of the daily Vishnu Sahasranama paath, and as the closing benediction of any Vaishnava bhajan session. (Sources: Vaishnava devotional tradition; Drikpanchang festivals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Achyutashtakam grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere daily recitation of the Achyutashtakam: (1) the cumulative punya of having uttered dozens of supreme Vishnu-names — since the Padma Purana, Bhagavata Purana and Vishnu Sahasranama Phala-shruti all promise immense merit to the chanter of Hari-namas; (2) Sarva-papa-pranashana — destruction of all sins, including the gravest pancha-mahapatakas, since each Hari-nama in the verses is a complete papa-nashana mantra; (3) the unified blessings of Krishna-bhakti (since the hymn opens with Krishna-Damodara-Gopika-vallabha) and Rama-bhakti (since verses 1, 4, 5 invoke Rama-Janaki-Lakshmana-Hanuman); (4) Bhaya-nashana — destruction of all fears, especially of death, since Achyuta is the never-falling protector; (5) Shanti — peace of mind through nama-japa-induced chitta-shuddhi; (6) Bhakti-vriddhi — growth of unwavering devotion to Vishnu in all his forms; (7) Mukti — final liberation as a Vaikuntha-vasi, as promised by Vishnu himself in the Bhagavad Gita to those who chant his name with devotion. (Source: classical Vaishnava nama-japa tradition.)
